It was Saturday, July 15 a week or so after Fourth of July and I poured out the last little bit of alcohol. I had in my house and decided never again.

I had a large task in front of me. I had to build a six figure company in about 30 days to be able to afford to live out here and fight for my daughter’s custody.

I had everything stripped from me. I had shut down all my other business ventures to go all in on this business with my ex-wife.

And so when she put me on the street, I essentially had to whip up a six figure business in 30 days. There was no other option she had burned the boats. It wasn’t even my choice.

And faced with that, I had two choices I could lean into vices and slow slowly wait to die

Or I could become a man let go of boyish things remove the devices from my life and become everything she told me I never could be

And I did I built the business to six figures and then seven I want custody of my daughter and I can proudly say I’m the best dad I’ve ever met

I dealt with hardships, slander and living across the country with no family or friends to help me navigate life as a simple father and a business founder at the same time

I don’t know how I did it, but I can tell you two things. It wouldn’t have happened with alcohol and it wouldn’t have happened without Jesus Christ.

Here’s to 1000 days sober

Ave Ave Christus Rex

#soberliving #sobriety #fitnesslife #businessowner #1000days

People think I don’t want to get married.

They’re wrong.

I want a wife who’s my best friend. Someone I can trust with my deepest fears, laugh with on the best days, and lean on during the worst ones. I want a peaceful home and a family built on loyalty, honesty, and commitment.

My standards are high.

Not because I’m looking for perfection, but because I’ve seen what happens when people choose the wrong partner.

The person you build a life with will influence your peace, your purpose, your children, your future… almost everything.

I’d rather wait a little longer for the right person than spend decades wishing I hadn’t settled.

Some decisions are simply too important to rush.

🚨PSA to all fitness coaches 🚨

I run a coaching program I’ve taken multiple coaches to $100,000 per month Let’s make one thing crystal clear.

The coaches hitting these numbers have been in business for years

That number does not come solely from ads

It takes 6 to 12 months for those ads to double the top line revenue of the business

If those ads are going to consistently pull in that same amount of revenue month of a month

every single coach who’s doing $100,000 to $200,000 a month in our program also has amazing organic content and they post extremely consistently

Being told to do one or the other like it is a magic fix is a lie and you should know better

Cutting out carbs was never the answer so neither is cutting out organic or cutting out ads

You need a well-rounded business strategy that you stick to for 6 to 12 months

A realistic goal over there 6 to 12 months should be to double what’s your business is pulling

If you’re lucky, you may be an outlier and 3 to 4X with your Business is pulling in

Create content for specific sides of the brain…

Here’s how

when you create content that focuses on the features and the benefits and the tangible tactical things that the client is getting

You’re selling to the right sided brain the analytical part of the brain that makes very well thought out decision decisions

This was solely attract people who are right brain thinkers, who are very analytical who will fight you on price take hours if not days to make decisions and will ultimately Syed with the cheapest option because that is what is most important to them

It’s why all of this work you’re doing to make your offer seem like it has all these bells and whistles ends up, bringing in the brokest leads

Conversely, if you sell to the left side of the human brain by using desired outcomes and story selling wrapped up in emotional analogies, you will begin to whip your viewers into a frenzy of desire and want

And this is powerful because this can be used for good, and this can definitely be used to scam and grift 

So to do this effectively and have a clear idea of what the true desired outcome is for the product that you’re selling

Then you want to lead with that desired outcome, while telling a story that illustrates the path to that desired outcome using your method

Do you want to over exaggerate how easy this was how superior it was to other options and the fact that this method is new indifferent compared to other things out there

Make it relatable by showing as many people as possible who will also gone through this method and achieve this result and also believe that it is superior irresistible new and different?

In doing so you will be selling hope to people who will pay money for more hope

Again, this can be used for good or evil so be careful with this information
I firmly believe there is a greater ROI in renting in a wealthy suburb vs buying a home in a shitty suburb.

Day 131: How to Not Be a Psychopath

If you know someone who says, “It is what it is,” trust me… they’re dangerous.

Not because they’ve given up.

Because they’ve stopped wasting energy trying to control things they never could.

Acceptance isn’t pretending you like what happened. It’s refusing to let every inconvenience steal your peace.

Most people spend their lives arguing with reality.

The strongest people accept it, solve what’s in front of them, and keep moving.

Protect your peace. Not your excuses.

If nobody’s told you this today, I love you. I believe in you. If you want to do hard things, you can do hard things. You got this. And come back tomorrow so we can all be a little less crazy together. I love y’all. Peace.

Day 114: How to Not Be a Psychopath

You don’t trust happiness.

You trust struggle.

A lot of people don’t realize they’ve spent so much time surviving that peace starts to feel suspicious.

The reading says that good feelings can actually make us anxious.

Not because something is wrong.

Because something is right.

And if you’ve spent years carrying stress, chaos, heartbreak, or uncertainty, joy can feel unfamiliar.

So when life finally starts getting better, you start looking for what’s wrong with it.

Maybe happiness isn’t the thing leaving.

Maybe you’re just not letting it stay.

Today’s reading is from The Language of Letting Go by Melody Beattie.

If nobody’s told you this today, I love you. I believe in you. If you want to do hard things, you can do hard things. You got this. And come back tomorrow so we can all be a little less crazy together.

I love y’all.

Peace.

Two key takeaways from this…

But first an apology I am a very objective thinker and so presented with facts I drop opinions and I lean into what’s actually real

And I can’t deny he gets real results for real coaches and they’re very happy (for the most part)

My first observation would be that this program is clearly designed for coaches who are already various established. You need to have the teams the SOP‘s and the systems in place for ads to be able to run and you need to have clear amounts of profit to be able to throw into ads on top of the investment you’re making for this program to work well for you.

Second, there is a difference between the results that someone gets and the way that person presents themselves online. I’m a big fan of the results that Will is getting his clients. I’m not a huge fan of how he voices that online and his attitude towards other coaches in the industry.

I wanna see this industry grow over the next 10 years and I wanna see it grow into a place that we’re all proud to be a part of 

So I still stand by what I said when it comes to how he is dealing with other coaches in the industry in their containers

I do not agree with attempting to poach clients from other coaches. I do not agree with talking poorly about other coaches and that’s why I’m making this video.

I don’t like that I took a shot at him, especially without having all the information. And clearly I am at a 10th of Will’s size and so I’m just punching up Will not punching down still isn’t a great look and I don’t think it’s the most productive use of any of our times

So while I stand by what I said when it came to how Will is dealing with other coaches

I do respect the results he gets for his clients and how he chooses to talk about those is completely up to him

With all that being said, let’s keep the focus on what we are all trying to do here which is impact more lives through Fitness

but thank you to all the clients from Will’s program who commented on the video and help me get a better understanding of what’s going on inside the container

“Gossiping about how I handled the worst year of my life instead of reaching out to see if I’m okay is actually wild.”

I’ve been thinking about that a lot lately.

If you really believed I was struggling… why wasn’t your first instinct to call me?

Why wasn’t it, “Hey man, are you okay? Do you need anything?”

Instead, the people who knew me best just… disappeared.

Some of them talked about me instead of talking to me.

And that’s the part I can’t wrap my head around.

Because if I saw someone I loved going through the hardest season of their life, I wouldn’t make them a conversation.

I’d make sure they weren’t carrying it alone.

That experience taught me something I’ll never forget.

Some people don’t leave because you’re hard to love.

They leave because compassion was never their first instinct.

Do you believe gear helps you sell fitness coaching?

I remember being so insecure about my size and how much muscle I had I truly believed that if I just got bigger, people would care

That I would be able to actually sell more coaching that a little bit more muscle was all that I needed to actually be respected as a man and until that happened I couldn’t be

hopping off of gear and focusing on building a healthy body as naturally as possible was the best decision of my life

My businesses did even better. My body felt even better for the first time I felt like a kid again I could run I could swim and surf all the things that I’d love doing growing up I could now do once more without feeling bloated and bogged down.

And my biggest concern, the fact that no one would like me if I wasn’t massive and jacked quickly faded away when I realized there’s so much more to being a good coach and a good leader than just having muscle

And most importantly, I was disrespecting the body that God gave me by blasting gear getting as big as possible, all in a selfish attempt to inflate my own ego and make as much money from it as possible

There’s nothing wrong with muscle there’s nothing wrong with money, but the way in which we go about obtaining both we need to watch carefully
We don’t run to be fit, we run for the fit.
Let’s. Talk. About. It….

Day 130: How to Not Be a Psychopath

You don’t miss them.

You miss feeling chosen.

That’s why it’s so easy to romanticize people who made you question your worth.

The love you deserve doesn’t keep you awake at night wondering where you stand. It brings peace, not anxiety. It helps you grow, not disappear. It reaches for your hand when life gets hard instead of letting go.

Stop confusing chemistry with safety.

Stop calling chaos passion.

The right relationship won’t make you question your value. It’ll make you wonder why you ever settled for less.

If nobody’s told you this today, I love you. I believe in you. If you want to do hard things, you can do hard things. You got this. And come back tomorrow so we can all be a little less crazy together. I love y’all. Peace.

Part 3 of 5 — You do not have to force pay-in-full to build a great coaching business.⁠
⁠
A lot of people treat the sale like the finish line.⁠
⁠
It’s not.⁠
⁠
The sale is the start.⁠
⁠
If your business is built around real retention, real results, and real relationships… then the goal isn’t just to collect cash.⁠
⁠
It’s to create customers who stay.⁠
⁠
People who keep getting value.⁠
⁠
People who keep winning.⁠
⁠
People who don’t want to leave because what you do actually works.⁠
⁠
That changes how you think about pricing.⁠
⁠
That changes how you think about renewal.⁠
⁠
That changes how you think about the whole business model.⁠
⁠
There isn’t one right way to structure offers.⁠
⁠
There’s only the way that creates the kind of business you actually want to own.⁠
⁠
Follow for part 4 tomorrow — we’re talking about why gatekeeping your best stuff is ancient history.

COACHING isn’t going anywhere

But these cheap info products have only a few months left to live

If you have ever touched AI, you understand that there is no reason for you to spend $10-$200 on somebody’s info product that can be answered with one prompt inside of Claude

And as more people adopt AI and get comfortable using it to learn, and to grow, they will realize this has always been a massive ruse

These products are always sold to people who did not have any insight into how to access information

They prayed on the innocence and the naivety of people just starting out

As more and more people understand the power of AI. They will realize that the only type of coaching they want to invest in his coaching where they get real access to real people to get live answers to the questions they have as they work along AI to build their business.

And trust me I’ve been behind the scenes on some of the largest info product programs in the market

There is nothing inside of these product that you could not get answered and implemented in 10 minutes inside of your business by using Claude

And if you don’t know how to use Claude, you can simply ask it. You don’t have to pay these people $27.

This is my 2027 prediction. The info product is up and the people who got rich off of it. We will watch them fall.
The people who had access to you had no idea how unreachable you are to other people until they became other people.
Get it together

Just because you’re attracted to someone doesn’t mean they’re meant for you.

Attraction can get you through the first date. It can’t carry a lifetime.

We’ve been taught to chase chemistry, but chemistry and chaos often wear the same disguise.

Instead of asking, “Do I want them?” try asking, “Who do I become when I’m with them?”

Do you feel peaceful? More yourself? More honest? Or do you feel like you have to earn their love?

You’re not just choosing someone to spend a Friday night with.

You’re choosing the person who might be sitting next to you on the worst day of your life.

Choose accordingly.

A magnetic business doesn’t drain your energy.

It multiplies it.
I mean if I keep getting 4x back I’m not stopping.
Make sure you know what you’re working for 🫡

Nobody cares about the features of your program

I could you now this is probably the number one reason why coaches do not make money

And it comes from an obsession with yourself versus an obsession with your customer

You care more about all the cool stuff you’re doing to your app that you never stopped to think does the person who’s going to buy this even care

You get so wrapped up in your own persona you lose sight of how to speak to the emotions that your prospect has and wants to have

Public apology:⁠
⁠
For years, I taught that once your offer went over $2k… you needed a sales call.⁠
⁠
Under $2k? Sure, sell it with a doc, a webinar, whatever.⁠
⁠
Over $2k? “Better get the closers in.”⁠
⁠
Turns out… that was complete rubbish.⁠
⁠
A client of mine had a $30k offer being sold in chat by VAs with their thumbs… while the “real” sales team was busy having feelings about it.⁠
⁠
That was the moment the penny dropped.⁠
⁠
If someone can sell a $30k offer without a call…⁠
⁠
maybe the call wasn’t the magic.⁠
⁠
Maybe the system was broken.⁠
⁠
So we tested it.⁠
⁠
Locked the sales team away.⁠
⁠
No calls.⁠
⁠
No chats.⁠
⁠
No closing.⁠
⁠
Nothing.⁠
⁠
15 months later?⁠
⁠
We’re selling 5x more every month than we did with a full sales team.⁠
⁠
That’s Salesless™.⁠
⁠

Treat yourself like a professional athlete. 

If you’re working, work. But if you’re not, get the fuck off your phone, turn off your computer, and relax.

So many creators burn themselves out not because they’re working exceptionally long hours, but because they have no real genuine down time. 

When they’re working they’re working, and when they’re not, they’re thinking about work, or doom scrolling Instagram, or watching YouTube videos about content. 

Imagine an athlete, who trained every hour of every day from the moment they woke up, to the moment they went to sleep. How well do you think that athlete would perform? 

Fucking terribly.

Well then why on early do you think you’d fair any better?

There is a direct correlation between my students who lack confidence & how vague and non specific their offer becomes. 

When someone lacks confidence in their ability to provide a specific outcome, they then think that leaving wiggle room in their offer will allow them to get away with not providing that outcome. 

But the issue is, people don’t pay for vague outcomes. They pay for certainty. 

And so ironically, your lack of confidence might be the reason you haven’t had the opportunity to build that confidence by having people choose to do business with you. 

It’s chicken of the egg. 

Either you’re willing to put your money where your mouth is, promise an outcome and then move heaven and earth to make sure your clients achieve it. 

Or you can be vague, make no money. 

Choice is yours.

I’ve worked with thousands of creators, and hundreds of them have asked for my vote of confidence in going for their first hire. Usually an editor, videographer or writer.

Here’s what I always tell them:

View hiring an experimental investment in your business, not a loss of income. 

Will this new person work out? Maybe, I don’t know. 

Will you be a better entrepreneur on the other side of making that initial investment? Absolutely.

So here’s what you can do to mitigate some of the nerves.

Set aside an investment stash, an amount of money you’re willing to see evaporate to test hiring. Maybe it’s $1,000, $10,000 or $100,000.

Set a timeline that gives you an escape hatch but also gives your new hire a chance to prove themselves (2-4 months I find is good)

Go in with the understanding that this will be a step back before it’s a step forward. People talk about hiring like it’s unlocking the secrets of the universe. It’s not, it’s going to suck at first. 

They’re not going to do things the way you want right out the gate. But not because they suck, but because you suck as a leader. 

You’re both investing in this partnership. 

So go in with a set amount of money, a set timeline, and an open mind, and you’ll undoubtedly come away better off. Either because you hired a weapon; or because you learnt a valuable lesson.

The moment you label someone as having something you don’t, you’ve put yourself down by comparison.

If you want to win, you need to recognize all the traits you admire in them, in yourself. And then recognize that there is literally no difference between them, and you.

I had a coach a few years ago, when I was first training for my Ironman give me a profound piece of advice; he said: 

“I can make you an elite triathlete, you just have to not get injured for 5+ years”

That’s business, most people get injured. 

They take a bet that goes south and run out of money. 

They work 16+ hour days and burn themselves out beyond reproach. 

When if they had have focused on longevity, they’d have ended up more successful. 

Now, that’s not to say you can’t work hard. 

You should absolutely work hard. But even elite athletes take recovery days. Even elite athletes periodize their training to manure volume and intensity. 

Even elite athletes understand where their limits are, and that understanding is what creates elite performance on race day. 

Treat yourself like an elite athlete. 

I’ve set some enormous goals for this year.

But I know, if my goals are that high, I need to manage my volume and intensity, just like I do with my training. 

And so, to manage the intensity of my goals, I have simultaneously:

- Decided to quit drinking alcohol for 2026
- Become incredibly protective of my weekends 
- Adopted a “no screens Sundays” plan 
- Created a “slow” morning routine so I actually don’t start work till 10am, but my work hours are now far more intense. 

All in an attempt to give myself the energy to push after my enormous goals. 

Long story short; if you’re an entrepreneur, pushing to build a 1% life, you are a high performer, treat yourself as such.
If the job requires a screwdriver, and you keep hitting the thing with a hammer, you’re gonna f*cking break the thing.

A brilliant idea that’s lightly held, will always be steamrolled by a poor idea that’s held with high conviction. 

Your audience wants to follow a leader; lead them.
Have some patience dude, it’s working, just not as quickly as you want it to.

I then started selling digital products for videographers; before I thought I was ready to.

Most of the time you’re going to downplay your expertise. If you’re asking the question, it means you care and if you care, you’re probably ready to help others.

I never thought I’d be a coach. But here I am.

We’re not in a “trust recession...”

Info products are not dying...

You just can’t get away with lying to people anymore.

Here’s a truth that has always been true, and will continue to be true till the end of time. 

“If you want value, create value.”

If you want to make millions, impact millions. 

In simple terms, to get what you want out of life, help others get what they want out of life. 

If you want a successful education business, start by focusing less on how to make yourself successful, and more on how to make your clients successful. 

2026 will be my biggest year financially that I’ve ever had in business, by a country f*cking mile. 

How do I know that? 

Because tippy top of my list of goals, is $20M in student wins. 

That’s not money in my pocket, no, that’s $20M in the pockets of my clients, created because of the value we created. 

And I’m fairly certain, if I can do that, I’ll be paid handsomely as a result.
You’re making things way harder than they need to be.
Success happens in the face of self doubt, not in the absence of it.

You’re not broken if your content, business or goals feel difficult. 

That’s normal.
Don’t get too caught up worrying about the finish line; that you forget to enjoy the race.

When someone wants your product, they know it could help them, they know they’re ready to invest, and then they learn that they can’t just buy it “whenever they’re ready.”

What do you think that does to their desire?

It drives them fucking crazy.

I wanted my first Rolex to commemorate getting engaged to Mekealy, so when I walked in, and was told I couldn’t buy it, I now NEEDED that Rolex.

Having a business where you control when people can buy, and more importantly WHO can buy, that is POWERFUL.

If you’re always available, what does that signal to your audience?

You’re a Breitling, or a Omega, or a Tudor. 

Not that those brands don’t make great watches, I love my Brietling... But it’s not a Rolex...

When I wear my Rolex’s, not only am I signalling success, accomplishment, and wealth... I’m signaling that I’m part of an exclusive club that most people aren’t allowed to be apart of.

So, what can you do to make your business more like Rolex?

If you can’t do it for a long time, it’s probably not worth doing for a short time.

All the biggest results you’ll achieve will be because of time, not effort. 

It’s the simple thing we all understand in theory, but in practice, ripping the willpower level as hard as we possibly can as often as we possibly can, feels like the right move. 

But here’s the issue.

Everything comes out in the wash. 

So if you put twice as much effort in now, eventually you’ll owe back your pound of flesh, and usually that comes in the form of burnout. 

Suddenly, any gains you temporarily made during that short stint of 2x effort need to be worth the downside you lose by not creating anything during your burnout. 

If you multiply that over years, who are you going to bet on?

The person who was super intense but had to disappear every 3-4 months whilst they were inevitably forced to recover from burnout?

Or the person who was half as intense; but remained consistent the entire time?

I’d be willing to wager it’s the latter.

So maybe, just maybe; the extra effort isn’t worth the squeeze, if it means you won’t be around to reap the big results that come with time. 

Believe me, I learnt this lesson the hard way.

Hard work doesn’t necessarily equal difficult work. 

The real key to success, finding the things that feel easy to you, and difficult to everyone else. Then working so eye wateringly hard at that thing that people think you’re an alien.
If you believe in what you’re selling, you’ll have no trouble selling it.

Choose wisely.

It was Saturday, July 15 a week or so after Fourth of July and I poured out the last little bit of alcohol. I had in my house and decided never again.

I had a large task in front of me. I had to build a six figure company in about 30 days to be able to afford to live out here and fight for my daughter’s custody.

I had everything stripped from me. I had shut down all my other business ventures to go all in on this business with my ex-wife.

And so when she put me on the street, I essentially had to whip up a six figure business in 30 days. There was no other option she had burned the boats. It wasn’t even my choice.

And faced with that, I had two choices I could lean into vices and slow slowly wait to die

Or I could become a man let go of boyish things remove the devices from my life and become everything she told me I never could be

And I did I built the business to six figures and then seven I want custody of my daughter and I can proudly say I’m the best dad I’ve ever met

I dealt with hardships, slander and living across the country with no family or friends to help me navigate life as a simple father and a business founder at the same time

I don’t know how I did it, but I can tell you two things. It wouldn’t have happened with alcohol and it wouldn’t have happened without Jesus Christ.

Here’s to 1000 days sober

Ave Ave Christus Rex

How long till it’s all in house?

There are a couple of coaches that I mentor who have already made their entire app themselves using Claude

And watching trainer eyes go down today for the entire day when most coaches are trying to do their check-in it made me think

How long until the majority of coaches figure out you can build an app with more customizations more tailored to your specific style of COACHING and have it functional all by just learning how to use Claude

It’s definitely a ways away, and I don’t think many people are good enough yet to know how to do it

But there are multiple coaches in my program right now who do you have their own apps and they are having a field day today watching everybody else scramble

You have to realize that AI is not for your carousels and it shouldn’t even be scripting your content

AI is for building infrastructure for your business that allows you to own all the pieces of it

And to build more inside of those business pieces to make your job easier less manual more effective

And if that interests you, you should definitely follow me here because I post all kinds of different builds for Fitness coaches

Create content for specific sides of the brain…

Here’s how

when you create content that focuses on the features and the benefits and the tangible tactical things that the client is getting

You’re selling to the right sided brain the analytical part of the brain that makes very well thought out decision decisions

This was solely attract people who are right brain thinkers, who are very analytical who will fight you on price take hours if not days to make decisions and will ultimately Syed with the cheapest option because that is what is most important to them

It’s why all of this work you’re doing to make your offer seem like it has all these bells and whistles ends up, bringing in the brokest leads

Conversely, if you sell to the left side of the human brain by using desired outcomes and story selling wrapped up in emotional analogies, you will begin to whip your viewers into a frenzy of desire and want

And this is powerful because this can be used for good, and this can definitely be used to scam and grift 

So to do this effectively and have a clear idea of what the true desired outcome is for the product that you’re selling

Then you want to lead with that desired outcome, while telling a story that illustrates the path to that desired outcome using your method

Do you want to over exaggerate how easy this was how superior it was to other options and the fact that this method is new indifferent compared to other things out there

Make it relatable by showing as many people as possible who will also gone through this method and achieve this result and also believe that it is superior irresistible new and different?

In doing so you will be selling hope to people who will pay money for more hope

Again, this can be used for good or evil so be careful with this information
This industry needs saving…

Building 7-figure businesses like legos

there’s a proven process it’s working right now and it’s all built out of AI

And while you’re following your favorite guru, who’s teaching you topics that helped him get his Lamborghini five years ago we’re out here helping people sign clients in real time

We’re not installing $1 million Funnel in your business in the next seven days

We’re not having a post six times a day dancing around your little screen

We’re implementing systems backed by a guy that are actually driving the needle forward

So you can keep opting into all those online gurus promising you the stars and delivering you nothing

Or you can actually start listening to the pros who are making seven figures the norm in 2026

-Will

Two key takeaways from this…

But first an apology I am a very objective thinker and so presented with facts I drop opinions and I lean into what’s actually real

And I can’t deny he gets real results for real coaches and they’re very happy (for the most part)

My first observation would be that this program is clearly designed for coaches who are already various established. You need to have the teams the SOP‘s and the systems in place for ads to be able to run and you need to have clear amounts of profit to be able to throw into ads on top of the investment you’re making for this program to work well for you.

Second, there is a difference between the results that someone gets and the way that person presents themselves online. I’m a big fan of the results that Will is getting his clients. I’m not a huge fan of how he voices that online and his attitude towards other coaches in the industry.

I wanna see this industry grow over the next 10 years and I wanna see it grow into a place that we’re all proud to be a part of 

So I still stand by what I said when it comes to how he is dealing with other coaches in the industry in their containers

I do not agree with attempting to poach clients from other coaches. I do not agree with talking poorly about other coaches and that’s why I’m making this video.

I don’t like that I took a shot at him, especially without having all the information. And clearly I am at a 10th of Will’s size and so I’m just punching up Will not punching down still isn’t a great look and I don’t think it’s the most productive use of any of our times

So while I stand by what I said when it came to how Will is dealing with other coaches

I do respect the results he gets for his clients and how he chooses to talk about those is completely up to him

With all that being said, let’s keep the focus on what we are all trying to do here which is impact more lives through Fitness

but thank you to all the clients from Will’s program who commented on the video and help me get a better understanding of what’s going on inside the container
Impact > Income = abundance

The minute you change this success flows effortlessly 

now the secret sauce to this is your actual belief in your offer. You truly have to believe you have something that is great and is worth that level of hype.

@therealbrianmark build his business over a decade and has thousands of case studies of success, which is why he’s able to have such conviction

But you don’t need to have such a high level of reputable success. All you need is one story, even your own that you truly believe in that you sell honestly that won’t give you the same level of conviction.

So stop trying to be everyone else stealing their content, rewriting their scripts searching for their hooks

Just start passionately sharing what makes you different. What makes you special what you love about your offer

And if you don’t know any of that yet, well great we have a $5000 course that we just made free

All you have to do to get access to it is comment “offer” and I’ll send you your logins

Do you believe gear helps you sell fitness coaching?

I remember being so insecure about my size and how much muscle I had I truly believed that if I just got bigger, people would care

That I would be able to actually sell more coaching that a little bit more muscle was all that I needed to actually be respected as a man and until that happened I couldn’t be

hopping off of gear and focusing on building a healthy body as naturally as possible was the best decision of my life

My businesses did even better. My body felt even better for the first time I felt like a kid again I could run I could swim and surf all the things that I’d love doing growing up I could now do once more without feeling bloated and bogged down.

And my biggest concern, the fact that no one would like me if I wasn’t massive and jacked quickly faded away when I realized there’s so much more to being a good coach and a good leader than just having muscle

And most importantly, I was disrespecting the body that God gave me by blasting gear getting as big as possible, all in a selfish attempt to inflate my own ego and make as much money from it as possible

There’s nothing wrong with muscle there’s nothing wrong with money, but the way in which we go about obtaining both we need to watch carefully

Nobody cares about the features of your program

I could you now this is probably the number one reason why coaches do not make money

And it comes from an obsession with yourself versus an obsession with your customer

You care more about all the cool stuff you’re doing to your app that you never stopped to think does the person who’s going to buy this even care

You get so wrapped up in your own persona you lose sight of how to speak to the emotions that your prospect has and wants to have

When you can get out out of your own way and stop carrying so much about yourself and how great you and your program are and you can be speaking to the fears, desires, and goals of your client

Then and only then will you start to see your program takeoff then and only then when people begin to buy from you in excess

But until you get out of your own way, you’ll continue talking about how great your program is compared to everybody else else’s will no one signs up

Most coaches who are struggling with content are not struggling because their ideas are bad or their production is lacking, they are struggling because they keep abandoning their message before it ever has a chance to land with the right person at the right time.

The instinct to constantly reinvent the strategy every few weeks resets the algorithm, confuses the audience, and kills the compounding effect that consistency creates over time.
People do not need to be taught something new nearly as much as they need to be reminded of what they already know but are not yet acting on.

The coach who shows up and hammers the same core message ten different ways over ninety days will always outperform the coach chasing a new idea every single day hoping something goes viral.
Repetition builds trust and trust is what converts a follower into a client.

Your ideal client needs to see your message multiple times before it lands at the exact moment they are finally ready to act, and if you switched topics last week you will not be there when that moment arrives.
Show up with the same message consistently, say it in different ways, and let the compounding do what one perfect post never could.

Most fitness coaches are broke not because they work with bad clients or deliver bad results but because they built an offer that sounds exactly like every other coach selling the same thing on the internet.

The S.I.N. Offer Framework™ fixes that.

S.I.N. stands for Specific, Irresistible, and Niche and it is the foundation of every high ticket online fitness coaching program that actually converts at scale. When your offer is specific it speaks directly to one person’s exact problem with language so accurate they feel like you wrote it for them. When it is irresistible the value so clearly outweighs the price that saying no feels like the irrational choice. When it is niche you are no longer competing in a crowded market because you have carved out a lane that belongs entirely to you.

The coaches scaling past $10k, $20k, even $50k per month are not doing it because they post more or run better ads. They are doing it because their offer is positioned so precisely that the content, the ads, and the conversations all pull in the same direction and the close feels inevitable.

If your DMs are quiet, your ads are bleeding money, or your discovery calls keep ending in maybes, the offer is the problem and the S.I.N. Framework is where you start rebuilding it the right way.

Most fitness coaches do not have a lead problem, they have a clarity problem.

The traffic is already there, people are landing on your page every single day.

But if they cannot tell within seconds exactly who you help and how you help them, they leave without a follow, without a DM, without ever entering your world.
This is why you see page views that never turn into followers, and followers that never turn into clients.

It is not an audience size issue and it is not a content volume issue.

It is a messaging issue, and it is costing you sales you do not even know you are losing.
When your profile speaks directly to one specific person and makes it undeniably clear what problem you solve and what they should do next, the whole funnel shifts.

Follows start turning into conversations, conversations turn into calls, and calls turn into clients because the right person immediately recognized that you were talking to them.
Profile clarity is not a branding exercise you do once and forget.

It is the foundation that every other part of your marketing sits on, and the coaches doing consistent revenue every month have this dialed in before they worry about anything else.

Fix the message first and the leads were always there waiting for it.

If your content is not bringing in clients consistently, the problem is almost never the volume of what you are posting or the quality of your production, it is what you are actually leading with and what you are asking people to buy into.

Posting workouts and selling programs is the default move for almost every fitness coach on the platform, and it is exactly why most of them are spinning their wheels with great engagement and zero clients.
Nobody wakes up wanting a program, they wake up wanting the result the program is supposed to create.

They want to feel confident with their shirt off, they want their energy back, they want to stop avoiding the mirror, they want their clothes to fit again, and when your content speaks to that outcome directly instead of the mechanism that delivers it, you attract a completely different type of attention.

The shift is simple but it changes everything.

Stop documenting the workout and start documenting the transformation it produced.

Stop selling the plan and start selling the life on the other side of the plan.

When someone scrolling your page sees themselves in the result you are describing and believes you are the person who can get them there, the conversation about working together starts itself.
Content that brings in clients is not more content, it is more specific, more outcome focused, and more honest about the real thing your ideal client actually wants.

Make that shift and watch how fast the right people start showing up in your DMs.

Here are the five IGA‘s. I’d be focusing on every day:

IGA 1 — Review Ads Dashboard and Flag One Optimization
Every morning. CPM, CPL, cost per call, close rate. Not to obsess but to own the numbers. One action item identified and either executed or delegated before noon. Ads treated as math not emotion.

IGA 2 — Post One Piece of Authority Content
Same as Academy. Never comes off the list. Authority Growth Funnel™ depends on consistent content output. At this stage the content should be driving warm inbound that reduces reliance on outbound.

IGA 3 — 5 High Value DM Conversations
Fewer but higher quality than Academy. These are warm leads, referrals, and inbound inquiries from content. The goal is to qualify, nurture, and move to call. Not volume but precision.

IGA 4 — One System Audit or Delegation Task
Identify one task they are still doing manually that should be automated or delegated. Document it. Build the SOP or the automation. This is the IGA that builds the business into a machine rather than a job. The Full-Time OS™ lives here.

IGA 5 — Client Results Check
One touchpoint per day with an active client. A GHL pipeline review, a Slack message, a quick win acknowledged. Retention and results drive referrals. At this stage referrals should be generating 20 to 30 percent of monthly revenue. If they are not, fulfillment is leaking.

Flag any tools they need to connect or accounts they need.
Estimate build time honestly.

The coaches who struggle to sign clients consistently are almost never struggling because they need more offers, more price points, or more options for people to choose from, they are struggling because not one of their existing offers solves a specific painful problem for one specific type of person with enough clarity to make the decision obvious.

More offers create more confusion, for you and for your prospect.

When someone lands on your page or gets on a call with you and they are trying to figure out which of your five things is right for them, you have already lost momentum and introduced doubt into a process that should feel simple and inevitable.

One offer built around one painful problem for one specific person changes everything.

You know exactly who you are talking to in every piece of content, every ad, every DM conversation, and every sales call because there is only one answer and it either fits or it does not.

That clarity in your marketing is what makes your ideal client feel like you built this specifically for them.
The best offer is not the most comprehensive one or the one with the most modules and bonuses.

It is the one where your ideal client reads the outcome and immediately thinks that is exactly what I need right now.

Nail that one thing first, sell it consistently, and build everything else on top of the foundation it creates.

If someone visits your Instagram page and cannot explain what you do and who you help in two sentences or less, you do not have a content problem or a follower problem, you have a profile problem and it is quietly killing your conversion rate every single day.

Your profile is the first thing every potential client sees before they ever watch a reel, read a caption, or send a DM, and if the message is not immediately obvious the majority of them will scroll past without a second thought because clarity is what earns the next five seconds of attention.

Most coaches make this mistake by trying to sound impressive instead of trying to sound specific.

A bio full of credentials, emojis, and vague transformation language tells the visitor almost nothing about whether this page is actually for them, and when people are unsure they leave.

The fix is not complicated but it requires you to get ruthlessly specific about who you help, what problem you solve, and what they should do next.

When those three things are undeniable from the first glance at your page, the right person stays, follows, and eventually reaches out because they immediately recognized themselves in your message.
Profile clarity is not a branding exercise you do once when you start your business.

It is the foundation your entire organic strategy is built on and it deserves the same attention you give your content.

Most coaches think scaling means sacrificing margin forever

It does not
Here is how we actually think about it
During a scaling push we run at 40 to 50% margin

We increase ad spend aggressively to hit a new revenue run rate

Say we are pushing from $150,000 to $200,000 a month
Ad spend goes up
Margin comes down
That is expected and intentional

Once we hit that new run rate we hold it for 3 months
Then we shift focus from growth to margin optimization

Same revenue
Better efficiency
Margin climbs back up

The goal for fitness coaching offers is 60 to 70% net margin
That is the number that gives you real freedom
Not just revenue on a screenshot
Actual profit you keep

Most coaches celebrate the top line and ignore what they take home
The move is to scale to a new level then tighten the machine until the margin reflects the revenue

Then do it again
This is how you build a fitness coaching business that actually pays you

Most marketing agencies will just tell you to film more ads

That is not a strategy
That is them buying time
Here is what they are missing about fitness specifically

Fitness is an intimate sale
People are not just buying a program
They are admitting they need help with something deeply personal

Think about what it actually takes for someone to raise their hand and say
I am an adult and I cannot control my own weight

That is embarrassing
That takes vulnerability

If your funnel sends them straight from an ad to a sales call with no relationship built in between
You are asking someone who is already ashamed to walk into a room and get sold
It is too big of an ask

The DM funnel changes that completely

Now there is a conversation
There is a human on the other side
There is trust being built before money ever gets mentioned

By the time the sale comes up it does not feel like a sale
It feels like the next logical step with someone they already trust

That is why fitness coaches specifically need to be running DM campaigns
Not just lead gen to a landing page

The intimacy of the conversation is the conversion mechanism

The thing no one can take away is your skill set and the thing that will allow you to buy the car over and over and over again the watch over and over and over again the house over and over and over again is your skills

And every time that monthly payment comes out, you realize it could’ve been going back into allowing you to go faster allow allowing you to get better

You also realize that the people who love you for the car and respect you for the car care for less about you than you do their perception of view matters nothing compared to your perception of you

The car will not fix your perception of yourself. You’re undoing commitment to get better every single day will.

When you could afford to scale, your skill set aggressively every single day in the car on top of that is pennies, then buy the car

But trust me as someone who’s been there and done it before you get obsessed with growing yourself you’re the Lamborghini become the Lamborghini first and then buy it

Competition is killing your joy in this exact framework 👇

I want you to imagine this for a second, imagine that you never knew how well anyone else was doing that for some reason you were cut off from the world your business still ran, but all you could see was your results

Think about how you’d act on a day-to-day basis there’s no one to compare yourself to. It’s just you getting it better every single day improving what you have what you’ve made the people you’re helping focusing on how they’re feeling the results. They’re getting the lives that you’re changing.

Never once getting pulled into a dopamine cycle of scrolling and comparing, never once talking down on yourself because you’re amazing results are less than somebody else is amazing results

Every single day ends with you going to bed feeling like you did everything you knew you could do to be better and the head that hits the pillow at night is a more evolved version than the head that raised in the morning

This is you outside of the competitive mindset this is you on the creative plane this is you when you take a quantum leap

This is coming from someone who’s in the process of doing that who’s failing miserably at it day today who’s struggling with the same struggles that you have who is scrolling on their phone at night comparing themselves to others just like you

A man who’s realizing there’s probably people who look at me the same way and think that they have to shrink because I’ve found success that they’ve had to find

We’re both wrong. You are going to be wildly successful independent of anything that I do and I’m gonna be wildly successful independent of anything anyone above me does.

The game is internal the field is quantum. The universe is abundant, and it is begging to give you everything that you want in this life if you will just attack each day with faith and gratitude.

Everyone talks about work/life balance like it’s something you achieve once and keep forever.

It’s not.

Balance isn’t static.

It’s a rhythm you have to find over and over again.

Some seasons require you to go all in on building.

Late nights. 
Deep focus. 
Sacrifice.

Other seasons require you to step back completely.

Rest.
Travel. 
Recharge.

The mistake most founders make is trying to split the difference every single day.

Half-working on vacation.

Half-present at dinner while checking Slack.

That’s not balance. That’s just being mediocre at both.

Real balance is knowing when to push and when to pull back.

When to build and when to live.

It’s not about perfection.

It’s about finding your rhythm and honoring it.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Slow down. Reflect. Recharge. 

That’s how you create a life and business that thrives.

AI can write your copy, build your funnels, and launch entire campaigns in minutes.

So execution isn’t the bottleneck anymore.

Taste is.

Taste is the ability to look at something and know it’s not good enough. Without anyone telling you.

You can just feel when something’s off or when it hits.

It’s the photography you choose. The words you cut. The spacing in your slides. The color grade. The shot you reframe because the first angle felt wrong.

That feeling people get when they see your brand? 

That’s taste.

And you develop it by what you consume.

If you’re scrolling mediocre content all day, your output will feel flat.

Study what’s great. Obsess over the small decisions that separate good from iconic.

AI will replace execution.

But it won’t replace the judgment to see what everyone else missed.

Taste is the only advantage that compounds.

Because execution gets commoditized.

Sound judgment never does.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Some of you keep chasing a new relationship when what you’re really chasing is relief.

It’s easier to blame the last girl. Easier to blame the timing, the chemistry, or the fact that she wasn’t “the one.”

But what if the real problem is that you’ve been asking another person to give you something you haven’t learned to give yourself?

That’s too much for anyone.

Build a life that excites you on your own. Take yourself on dates. Find hobbies. Chase goals. Learn to enjoy your own company.

Because if you’re constantly looking for someone else to rescue you from loneliness, you’ll mistake temporary relief for love every single time.

The person you choose to build a life with will influence your peace more than almost any decision you’ll ever make.

Chemistry is exciting.

Character is what carries you through the hard days.

Pay attention to how they handle conflict. How they treat people when nobody’s watching. Whether they take accountability, tell the truth, and stay loyal when life gets difficult.

Don’t just choose someone you’re attracted to.

Choose someone whose character makes you feel safe.

Because your partner can make the next forty years feel like home… or like a prison.

Some people spend so much time asking why their partner isn’t more affectionate, more vulnerable, or more emotionally open.

But very few stop to ask whether they’ve created an environment where those things can actually exist.

Emotional safety isn’t about always saying the right thing. It’s about being the kind of person someone doesn’t have to protect themselves from.

Can they tell you they’re hurting without being judged?

Can they admit they’re scared without being dismissed?

Can they be honest without worrying it’ll be used against them in the next argument?

The softest parts of people don’t come out because they’re demanded. They come out because they’re safe.

Before you ask why someone won’t let their guard down, ask yourself whether you’ve given them a reason to.

There are seasons in life where you don’t feel like the old version of yourself anymore… but you haven’t become the new version yet either.

That’s where I’ve been.

From the outside, it probably looks like everything is going great. The business is growing. The content is growing. Life looks put together.

But behind the scenes, I’ve been questioning a lot.

250,000 followers in six and a half months.

It’s funny. I thought this number would feel different.

For months I’ve been so locked in on one million that when I crossed 250k, I almost skipped right past it. But I forced myself to stop for a second and feel grateful.

There are a handful of people who have had a massive impact on this journey. The people tagged below have challenged me, inspired me, believed in me, and helped shape the creator I’m becoming. Thank you. @notreallywellness @higherupwellness  @jacksdayinthelife  @jb_copeland 

This journey has cost me, too. I’ve lost friendships, or at least people I thought were friends. But in exchange, I’ve found something far more valuable: purpose.

I’ve learned that when you believe in what you’re building, setbacks become fuel. Criticism becomes noise. The only thing left to do is keep showing up.

If there’s something on your heart that you know you’re capable of accomplishing, don’t let anyone convince you to shrink it. Bet on yourself. Then prove yourself right.

One million isn’t a dream anymore. It’s an appointment.

You’re going to have to put me in a casket because I’m not stopping until I get there.

Not so they regret losing you, but so you become the man you promised yourself you’d be.

The goal was never to prove them wrong.

The goal was always to prove yourself right.

But if I’m being transparent, that doesn’t mean I never question it.

I’m 36 years old. There was a time I didn’t think I’d make it past 20. I thought I’d end up dead, in prison, or just another statistic. Instead, I get to come home to a life I once prayed for.

Some nights, though, it’s quiet.

And my mind starts asking questions.

Am I being too patient?

Too picky?

Should I have stayed with someone from my past?

Am I starting this chapter too late?

Will I be old when my kids grow up? Will I get to meet my grandchildren?

Those thoughts are real.

But when I stop reacting emotionally and look at the facts, I realize something.

Every relationship has brought out a better version of me than the one before it.

I’ve become more patient. More emotionally available. More self-aware. More capable of loving someone well.

So maybe none of those relationships were failures.

Maybe they were preparing me to become the husband my future wife deserves.

I don’t know exactly when she’ll show up.

I just know that when she does, I want to be the kind of man who’s ready.

Maybe it’s time to talk about it

People assume I’ve given up on marriage.

That couldn’t be further from the truth.

I want the kind of relationship where my wife is also my closest friend. Someone I can be completely honest with. Someone I can celebrate life’s highs with and depend on when everything falls apart. I want a home that feels peaceful, a marriage built on trust, and a family rooted in love, loyalty, and integrity.

My standards aren’t high because I’m chasing perfection.

The person you choose will shape your everyday life. They’ll influence your peace of mind, the way you grow, the environment your children are raised in, and the direction of your future.

If waiting means I have a better chance of building that kind of life, then waiting is a price I’m happy to pay.

Dating at 36…

For a long time, I thought I had trust issues.

The truth is, I kept choosing people who turned my vulnerability into ammunition.

So I stopped opening up. I stopped trusting. I convinced myself that staying guarded was safer.

But I’ve realized something:

The answer isn’t becoming cold. The answer is becoming selective.

The wrong people may have taught me caution, but they don’t get to teach me how to love.

I still believe in connection. I still believe in vulnerability. I still believe there are people worth opening up to.

Fear doesn’t get to make that decision for me.

Live by the fit die by the fit

Love isn’t finding someone who could never hurt you.

It’s finding someone who could… and chooses not to.

Every relationship comes with risk. The moment you let someone into your life, you’re giving them access to the parts of you that nobody else gets to see. They now have the ability to build you up or break you down.

That’s why trust matters more than chemistry.

Chemistry can be instant. Trust is earned. Chemistry creates butterflies. Trust creates peace.

The healthiest relationships aren’t built on the absence of vulnerability. They’re built on the confidence that your vulnerability will be handled with care.

Maybe that’s what love really is.

Not certainty.

Just the courage to be fully seen by someone you’ve decided is worth trusting.

Day 122: How to Not Be a Psychopath

You’re not waiting for the right time.

You’re waiting until it feels comfortable.

The problem is… it never will.

Today’s reading reminded me of something Marcus Aurelius wrote:

“The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.”

The obstacle isn’t stopping you.

It’s shaping you.

Every uncomfortable conversation, every rejection, every setback, and every failure is an invitation to become someone stronger. The path you’re trying to avoid might be the exact path you’ve been praying for.

Stop asking life to get easier.

Start becoming harder to break.

If nobody’s told you this today, I love you. I believe in you. If you wanna do hard shit, you can do hard shit. You got this. And come back tomorrow so we can all be a little less crazy together. I love y’all. Peace.
Why are you really dating?

I’m 36 years old. Single. No kids.

People assume that means I’ve given up on relationships.

They’re wrong.

I want a wife. I want my best friend. I want a peaceful home and a family.

But I’ve also been in relationships where I was convinced I’d found “the one.” Looking back, I realized my picker was broken.

It took years of therapy, heartbreak, books, podcasts, journaling, and some very uncomfortable conversations with myself to become someone who’s finally okay being alone.

And I’m proud of that.

Being at peace with where you are doesn’t mean you’ve stopped believing in where you’re going.

I’d rather spend a few extra years becoming the right partner than spend decades with the wrong one.

If nobody’s told you this today, I love you. I believe in you. If you want to do hard things, you can do hard things. You got this. And come back tomorrow so we can all be a little less crazy together. I love y’all. Peace.

Your children don’t get to choose their parents.

But you get to choose who you have children with.

That’s one of the biggest decisions you’ll ever make.

Your partner won’t just influence your happiness. They’ll shape your home, your values, your children’s childhood, and the example they’ll carry into adulthood.

Chemistry fades. Looks change. Life gets hard.

Character is what remains.

Before asking, “Do I love this person?” ask yourself something even more important:

Would I want my future children to become like them?

That question has the power to change everything.

This one is for the over thinkers.

Too many people stay in relationships because they feel chosen.

They make you feel peaceful.

They make you feel accepted.

They make you feel more like yourself, not less.

You are just other people now

There are seasons in life where you don’t feel like the old version of yourself anymore… but you haven’t become the new version yet either.

That’s where I’ve been.

From the outside, it probably looks like everything is going great. The business is growing. The content is growing. Life looks put together.

But behind the scenes, I’ve been questioning a lot.

I’ve lost people I thought would be around forever. I’ve had to let go of parts of my life that no longer fit. And I’m trying to trust something I can’t fully see yet.

It’s a strange feeling. You know you can’t go backward, but moving forward feels uncertain.

I don’t have all the answers.

I’m still figuring it out.

Maybe growth isn’t feeling confident all the time. Maybe it’s continuing to move even when you don’t know exactly where the path leads.

If you’ve been feeling like you’re caught between the person you were and the person you’re becoming, maybe you’re closer than you think.

Sometimes the old life has to fall apart before the new one has room to exist.

Discipline isn’t always about becoming a better version of yourself.

Sometimes it’s about refusing to become the version of yourself you worked so hard to leave behind.

I don’t wake up every day excited to run, work, eat well, or have the difficult conversations. I do those things because I’ve seen what my life looks like when I stop keeping promises to myself.

The truth is, you rarely lose your life all at once. You lose it one small compromise at a time. One skipped workout. One excuse. One unhealthy habit. One decision you convince yourself doesn’t matter.

The same is true in the other direction.

You build a life you’re proud of one promise kept at a time.

For me, discipline isn’t about chasing perfection. It’s about protecting the peace, purpose, and life I fought to build.

If nobody’s told you this today, I love you. I believe in you. If you wanna do hard things, you can do hard things. You got this. Come back tomorrow so we can all be a little less crazy together. Peace.

Day 116: How to Not Be a Psychopath

You hate yourself.

Or at least that’s what they taught you.

The way you speak to yourself didn’t come out of nowhere.

Somewhere along the way, you learned that mistakes made you unworthy, perfection was expected, or love had to be earned.

The reading reminded me of something powerful:

We often end up treating ourselves the way we were treated.

But if that voice was learned…

It can be unlearned.

The next time you catch yourself tearing yourself apart, ask one question:

Would I ever say this to someone I love?

If the answer is no, stop saying it to yourself.

Today’s reading is from The Language of Letting Go by Melody Beattie.

If nobody’s told you this today, I love you. I believe in you. If you want to do hard things, you can do hard things. You got this. And come back tomorrow so we can all be a little less crazy together.

I love y’all.

Peace.

People think you’re okay because you’re functioning.

But functioning and healing are not the same thing.

After I lost my dog, I learned that grief doesn’t always look like falling apart. Sometimes it looks like showing up. Taking meetings. Building your business. Smiling when your heart is breaking.

More recently, I had two people I considered my best friends walk out of my life. That grief still shows up too.

The hard part is that life keeps moving. The work still needs to get done. The camera still turns on.

And when you’re the person who keeps showing up, people assume you’re fine.

Sometimes you’re not.

Sometimes you’re just doing your best to carry the weight.

If you’re carrying something heavy right now and still finding a way to move forward, keep going. The people you’ve lost wouldn’t want their absence to become the reason you stop becoming who you’re meant to be.
Life is Good ❤️

Success might not be something you have to chase.

Maybe it’s something that naturally follows when your life is aligned with who God created you to be.

A bird doesn’t fail because it can’t swim. A fish isn’t broken because it can’t fly. They’re only limited when they’re in an environment they were never designed for.

I wonder how many people have convinced themselves they’re untalented or incapable, when the real problem isn’t them. It’s where they’ve been trying to build their life.

The goal isn’t to become someone else.

The goal is to become exactly who you were created to be.
Live by the fit die by the fit

One person’s inability to recognize your value does not lower your value.

The relationship might be over, but sometimes the attachment lingers long after it’s gone. That’s the part that hurts.

Stop revisiting what already ended. Stop looking for closure in places that have already given you an answer.

Put that energy back into yourself. Build your body. Build your mind. Build your life.

One day you’ll realize you weren’t losing the right person.

You were making room for them.

We’ve been taught to chase fireworks.

The butterflies. The obsession. The instant chemistry. The feeling that you just “know.”

But I’ve started wondering if that’s exactly what keeps so many of us stuck.

Healthy relationships aren’t built on emotional whiplash. They’re built on consistency. On choosing each other over and over again. On watching trust compound over months and years.

The crazy highs only exist because they’re attached to equally devastating lows.

Real love doesn’t have to convince you it’s real every other day.

It grows quietly.

And one day you look back and realize the life you’ve built together creates a deeper happiness than any spark ever could.

Maybe we’ve been confusing intensity with intimacy.

They’re not the same thing.

The person you choose to build a life with will influence your peace more than almost any decision you’ll ever make.

Chemistry is exciting.

Character is what carries you through the hard days.

Pay attention to how they handle conflict. How they treat people when nobody’s watching. Whether they take accountability, tell the truth, and stay loyal when life gets difficult.

Don’t just choose someone you’re attracted to.

Choose someone whose character makes you feel safe.

Because your partner can make the next forty years feel like home… or like a prison.
I think it’s time to crash out…spiritually.

Some of you keep chasing a new relationship when what you’re really chasing is relief.

It’s easier to blame the last girl. Easier to blame the timing, the chemistry, or the fact that she wasn’t “the one.”

But what if the real problem is that you’ve been asking another person to give you something you haven’t learned to give yourself?

A relationship can add to your happiness.

It cannot become the source of it.

No partner can carry the weight of being your purpose, your peace, your confidence, your entertainment, and your identity all at once.

That’s too much for anyone.

Build a life that excites you on your own. Take yourself on dates. Find hobbies. Chase goals. Learn to enjoy your own company.

Because if you’re constantly looking for someone else to rescue you from loneliness, you’ll mistake temporary relief for love every single time.

The healthiest relationships aren’t built by two people trying to complete each other.

They’re built by two people who already enjoy the lives they’ve created and decide to share them.
That’s the lie they told you to believe
My favorite American pastime @sesh

The only family member you’ll ever get to choose is your partner.

So why do people rush the biggest decision of their lives?

My therapist once told me, “Your children don’t get to choose their parents. But you absolutely get to choose who their mother is.”

That perspective changed everything for me.

People give me a hard time for being 36 and still single. That’s okay. I’d rather take my time choosing the person I’ll build a family with than spend decades wondering if I settled too soon.

Nobody is perfect. But before you commit to forever, I think you owe it to yourself to truly know who you are… and who they are.

The person you marry won’t just know who you are today.

They’ll meet every version of you that life creates.

The version who succeeds.
The version who fails.
The version who’s grieving.
The version who’s healing.
The version who has no idea what they’re doing.

Marriage isn’t just choosing someone you love today.

It’s choosing the person you trust to walk beside every future version of yourself.

That’s why character matters more than chemistry.

Some decisions don’t just change your life.

They shape every chapter that comes after it.

The conversation isn’t whether every woman wants the same man.

The real question is… why wouldn’t she want someone who’s healthy, disciplined, emotionally mature, confident, and living with purpose?

Too many men spend their energy complaining about competition instead of becoming competitive.

Build your body. Build your character. Build your career. Heal your past.

The goal was never to become “the top 1%.”

The goal is to become the best version of yourself.

And that’s the version people are naturally drawn to.

My addiction convinced me my life was worthless.

Not because someone else said it. Because addiction slowly changes the way you see yourself.

Every lie, every broken promise, every person you disappoint becomes more “proof” that you’re beyond saving. Eventually, you stop believing you made bad choices and start believing you are the bad choice.

That’s the cruelest part of addiction. It doesn’t just steal your time or your relationships. It steals your identity.

But addiction is a liar.

If you’re reading this and you’re in that place today, don’t believe everything your addiction tells you. The voice telling you you’re too broken, too far gone, or beyond redemption is lying.

There is a life on the other side of this. I know because I’m living it.
Life is really good man ❤️

Do you actually want a relationship… or do you just like the idea of one?

A healthy relationship doesn’t just require love. It requires capacity.

Time. Energy. Presence. Consistency.

Can you make someone a priority when you’re exhausted? When work is overwhelming? When life gets messy?

Most people spend all their time looking for the right partner.

Very few stop to ask whether they’re ready to be one.

Sometimes the biggest thing standing between you and the relationship you want isn’t finding the right person.

It’s becoming someone with the capacity to build it.

People see the freedom.

They see the business, the followers, the peace, the lifestyle.

What they don’t see are the sleepless nights, the failed relationships, the therapy, the jail cell, the addiction, the mistakes, or the thousands of small decisions it took to rebuild my life.

Most people aren’t jealous of your journey.

They’re jealous of your destination.

Be careful comparing your Chapter 2 to someone else’s Chapter 20. Every life has a price that was paid behind the scenes.

Keep building yours. One day, someone will look at your life and wish they had what you have, without realizing what it took to get there.
Gossiping about that period of my life and walking out the door instead of calling your friends and checking on them will never make sense to me
